skill.name: ship-learn-next
skill.description: Turn transcripts, articles, tutorials, and course notes into concrete Ship-Learn-Next plans with shippable reps, reflection prompts, and next steps.
skill.summary: Convert learning material into a practical action plan built around shipping, reflection, and iteration.
skill.author: softaworks

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged shell execution, weak crypto, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found those hits in Markdown examples, filenames, and instructional text rather than executable code. The skill has legitimate Read and Write permissions and saves Markdown plans, so the remaining risk is limited workspace file access.

low_findings.title: Markdown Plan File Writes
low_findings.description: The skill can read user-provided learning material and write a generated Markdown plan. This is expected behavior, but users should review the destination filename before saving to avoid overwriting workspace files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed tools include Read and Write, and the instructions explicitly require saving a Markdown plan. The behavior is legitimate for this skill, but it is still a real filesystem permission concern.

content.user_title: Turn Learning Content Into Action Plans
content.value_statement: Learning content is easy to consume but hard to apply. This skill converts lessons into small shippable reps, reflection prompts, and next actions.
actual_capabilities: Reads transcripts, articles, tutorials, and course notes provided by the user.
Extracts actionable lessons instead of broad summaries.
Defines a practical quest with a clear 4-8 week target.
Creates Rep 1 with ship goals, success criteria, action steps, and reflection questions.
Maps future reps that build on what the user learns.
Saves the plan as a Markdown file using a consistent filename format.
limitations: It does not verify whether the source advice is accurate or safe.
It depends on the quality and completeness of the provided learning material.
It cannot confirm the user completed each rep without follow-up input.
It writes Markdown plans and does not manage external task systems.

use_cases.title: Apply a Tutorial
use_cases.description: Convert a watched tutorial into a first project, success criteria, and next practice reps.

use_cases.title: Turn Course Notes Into Practice
use_cases.description: Use class notes to define a small artifact to build this week and a reflection loop after shipping.

use_cases.title: Operationalize Business Advice
use_cases.description: Translate an article or transcript into concrete experiments, timelines, and measurable outcomes.

prompt_templates.title: Make This Actionable
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with one learning source.
prompt_templates.prompt: Read this transcript and turn it into a Ship-Learn-Next plan. Help me choose Rep 1 for this week.

prompt_templates.title: Build From Course Notes
prompt_templates.scenario: User has notes and a broad goal.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use these course notes to create a 4-week quest. Focus on one shippable artifact and practical reps.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Progressive Reps
prompt_templates.prompt: My goal is to reach this outcome in 6 weeks. Extract the useful lessons and design reps 1 through 5.

prompt_templates.title: Reflect And Iterate
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced follow-up after completing a rep.
prompt_templates.prompt: I shipped Rep 1 and here is what happened. Help me reflect, identify lessons, and plan Rep 2.

output_examples.input: A cold email outreach transcript and a goal to book meetings.
output: A quest goal focused on booking meetings within six weeks.
Rep 1: send ten targeted emails by Friday.
Reflection questions about responses, copy quality, targeting, and next improvements.

output_examples.input: React course notes and a goal to build a portfolio.
output: A first rep that ships a single-page portfolio with one project.
Success criteria covering deployment, visible content, and one working interaction.
Future reps that add styling, data, and feedback from real viewers.

output_examples.input: An article about testing proven markets.
output: A plan to test one market with a small landing page or outreach experiment.
Minimal resources needed before shipping the first test.
A follow-up path based on user responses and evidence gathered.

best_practices: Provide the full source material and state the outcome you want in 4-8 weeks.
Keep Rep 1 small enough to complete in 1-7 days.
Return after each shipped rep with real results so the next plan can improve.
anti_patterns: Using the skill to create a long study schedule without a shippable artifact.
Adding many resources before completing the first rep.
Skipping reflection and asking for the next plan without evidence from the last rep.

faq.question: What does this skill produce?
faq.answer: It produces a Markdown Ship-Learn-Next plan with a quest, reps, action steps, and reflection questions.

faq.question: What input works best?
faq.answer: Transcripts, articles, tutorials, and course notes work best when they contain practical advice or examples.

faq.question: Does it summarize the whole source?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on actionable lessons that can become small practice reps.

faq.question: Can it help after I finish Rep 1?
faq.answer: Yes. Share what happened, and it can help reflect and design the next rep.

faq.question: Does it need external services?
faq.answer: No. The skill uses local reading and Markdown file writing, with no required network service.

faq.question: Can it replace expert advice?
faq.answer: No. It organizes action steps, but users should verify any domain-specific advice before applying it.
